= Dazdursk Amberbluff =

{{CharacterInfobox
| name        = Dazdursk Amberbluff
| portrait    = https://foundry.atkennedy.com/modules/pf2e-tokens-characters/assets/portraits/kulenett-dwarf.webp
| level       = 8
| size        = Medium
| hp          = 0/0
| ac          = 24
| perception  = +15
| speed       = 25 ft.
| fortitude   = +15
| reflex      = +16
| will        = +13
| attributes  = '''STR''' +3 | '''DEX''' +4 | '''CON''' +3 | '''INT''' +0 | '''WIS''' +3 | '''CHA''' -1
| key_ability  = STR
| hero_points  = 0
| last_kill   = Gilded Gunner Assassin (2026-02-16)
| last_downed = Pyronite Ooze (2026-05-18)
}}

== Details ==
* '''Ancestry:''' Dwarf
* '''Heritage:''' <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/features/ancestry/strong-blooded-dwarf.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Strong-Blooded Dwarf" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Strong-Blooded Dwarf
* '''Background:''' Gunsmith
* '''Class:''' <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/classes/gunslinger.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Gunslinger" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Gunslinger
* '''Key Ability:''' STR
* '''XP:''' 575

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>⚔ Defense & Saves</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
* '''HP:''' 0/0
* '''AC:''' 24
* '''Perception:''' +15 ({{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}})
* '''Speed:''' 25 ft. (land)
* '''Fortitude:''' +15 ({{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}})
* '''Reflex:''' +16 ({{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}})
* '''Will:''' +13 ({{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}})
* '''Active Conditions:''' Dying 4, Clumsy 2, Blinded, Wounded 1, Unconscious, Prone
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>📊 Ability Scores</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; width:100%;"
|-
! STR !! DEX !! CON !! INT !! WIS !! CHA
|-
| +3 || +4 || +3 || +0 || +3 || -1
|}
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>🎓 Skills</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="width:100%;"
! Skill !! Modifier !! Proficiency
|-
| Acrobatics || +18 || {{color|#8a2be2|'''Master'''}}
|-
| Arcana || +0 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Athletics || +13 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Crafting || +10 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Deception || -1 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Diplomacy || -1 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Engineering Lore || +0 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Intimidation || -1 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Medicine || +15 || {{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}}
|-
| Nature || +3 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Occultism || +0 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Performance || -1 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Religion || +3 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Society || +0 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Stealth || +14 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Survival || +13 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Thievery || +14 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|}
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>💰 Wealth & Carry</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
{| class="wikitable"
! Coin !! Total Value !! Bulk !! Invested
|-
| None || 0 gp || 1.3 / 8 enc / 13 max || 0/10
|}
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>✨ Feats & Features</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
=====Heritage=====
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/features/ancestry/strong-blooded-dwarf.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Strong-Blooded Dwarf" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Strong-Blooded Dwarf'''
: Your blood runs hearty and strong, and you can shake off toxins. You gain poison resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1), and each of your successful saving throws against a poison affliction reduces its stage by 2, or by 1 for a virulent poison. Each critical success against an ongoing poison reduces its stage by 3, or by 2 for a virulent poison.

=====Background Features=====
; '''Gunsmith'''
: From a young age, you apprenticed to an experienced gunsmith and over time your skill and knowledge rose to match that of your master. Today, you can easily repair, modify, or improve nearly any firearm you've seen before, and even if you haven't seen it, you'll break it down to learn about what makes it tick in no time. You've taken those skills on the road, using them to assist yourself and your allies as an adventurer when it comes to maintaining their weapons, shields, armor, and more.

Choose two attribute boosts. One of these boosts must be to '''Intelligence''' or '''Dexterity''', and one is a free attribute boost.

You're trained in the Crafting skill and the Engineering Lore skill. You gain the Quick Repair skill feat.

=====Feats=====
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Battle Medicine" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Battle Medicine''' <small>(general, healing, manipulate, skill)</small>
: '''Requirements''' You're holding or wearing a healer's toolkit.

You can patch up wounds, even in combat. Attempt a Medicine check with the same DC as for Treat Wounds and restore the corresponding amount of HP; this doesn't remove the wounded condition. As with Treat Wounds, you can attempt checks against higher DCs if you have the minimum proficiency rank. The target is then immune to your Battle Medicine for 1 day. This does not make them immune to, or otherwise count as, Treat Wounds.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Blast Resistance" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Blast Resistance''' <small>(dwarf)</small>
: '''Access''' You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

You're used to the clamor of steel, the crack of a firearm, and the thunderous blast of explosions. You gain resistance 3 to fire and sonic. If you roll a success on a saving throw against an effect that causes the Dazzled or Deafened conditions, you get a critical success instead.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/weapons/clan-dagger.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Clan Dagger" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Clan Dagger''' <small>(dwarf)</small>
: You get one Clan Dagger of your clan for free, as it was given to you at birth. Selling this dagger is a terrible taboo and earns you the disdain of other dwarves.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Clan Pistol" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Clan Pistol''' <small>(dwarf)</small>
: '''Access''' You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

Raised in the ancient halls of Dongun Hold or the surrounding region, you display your lineage with a clan pistol, rather than a clan dagger. You get one clan pistol of your clan for free, as it was given to you at birth. This replaces your clan dagger. Selling this pistol is a terrible taboo and earns you the disdain of other dwarves. You are trained with the clan pistol. In addition, when your clan pistol is visible, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus on checks to Gather Information or Make an Impression when interacting with citizens of Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, or their allies.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Continual Recovery" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Continual Recovery''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: You zealously monitor a patient's progress to administer treatment faster. When you Treat Wounds, your patient becomes immune for only 10 minutes instead of 1 hour. This applies only to your Treat Wounds activities, not any other the patient receives.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Drifter's Juke" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Drifter's Juke''' <small>(flourish, gunslinger)</small>
: '''Requirements''' You're wielding a firearm or crossbow in one hand, and your other hand is either wielding a melee weapon or is empty.

You move in and out of range to complement your attacks. You Step, make a Strike, Step, and make another Strike. One Strike must be a ranged Strike using your firearm or crossbow, and the other must be a melee Strike using your melee weapon or unarmed attack. You can choose not to take one or both Steps.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Dwarven Weapon Familiarity" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Dwarven Weapon Familiarity''' <small>(dwarf)</small>
: Your kin have instilled in you an affinity for hard-hitting weapons, and you prefer these to more elegant arms. You gain access to all uncommon weapons with the dwarf trait. You have familiarity with weapons with the dwarf trait plus the battle axe, pick, and warhammer—for the purposes of proficiency, you treat any of these that are martial weapons as simple weapons and any that are advanced weapons as martial weapons.

At 5th level, whenever you get a critical hit with one of these weapons, you get its critical specialization effect.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Fake Out" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Fake Out''' <small>(gunslinger, visual)</small>
: '''Trigger''' An ally is about to use an action that requires an attack roll, targeting a creature within your weapon's first range increment.

'''Requirements''' You're wielding a loaded firearm or crossbow.

With a skilled flourish of your weapon, you force an enemy to acknowledge you as a threat. Make an attack roll to Aid the triggering attack. If you dealt damage to that enemy with the same weapon since the start of your last turn, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to this roll.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Fleet" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Fleet''' <small>(general)</small>
: You move more quickly on foot. Your Speed increases by 5 feet.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Grit and Tenacity" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Grit and Tenacity''' <small>(fortune, gunslinger)</small>
: '''Frequency''' once per hour

'''Trigger''' You fail a Fortitude or Will save.

You call upon deep reserves of toughness and mental fortitude to power through an otherwise debilitating effect. Reroll the triggering save with a +2 circumstance bonus; you must use the second result.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/features/classes/gunslinger-weapon-mastery.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Gunslinger Weapon Mastery" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Gunslinger Weapon Mastery''' <small>(gunslinger)</small>
: You fully understand the best way to utilize your unique weapons. Your proficiency rank increases to master with simple and martial firearms and crossbows. Your proficiency rank for advanced firearms and crossbows, simple weapons, martial weapons, and unarmed attacks increases to expert. You gain access to the critical specialization effects for firearms and crossbows.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/symbols/question-stone-yellow.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Gunslinger's Way" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Gunslinger's Way''' <small>(gunslinger)</small>
: All gunslingers have a particular way they follow, a combination of philosophy and combat style that defines both how they fight and the weapons they excel with. At 1st level, your way grants you an initial deed, a unique reload action called a slinger's reload, and proficiency with a particular skill. You also gain advanced and greater deeds at later levels, as well as access to way-specific feats.

'''Way of the Drifter''' You wander across the battlefield, equally at home in and out of the fray.

'''Way of the Pistolero''' You carefully maintain your distance at a duelist's 10 paces with pistol in hand.

'''Way of the Sniper''' You practice a methodical style of shooting, striking from far cover.

'''Way of the Vanguard''' You rely on a brutal combat style that uses heavy weapons and explosive attacks.

'''Way of the Triggerbrand''' You prefer firearms that work well as weapons in both melee and ranged combat
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/perception/eye-tendrils-web-purple.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Perception Mastery" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Perception Mastery''' <small>(barbarian, bard, commander, exemplar, gunslinger, investigator, ranger, rogue, swashbuckler, thaumaturge)</small>
: '''Barbarian''' Your instinct heightens each of your senses further. Your proficiency rank for Perception increases to master.

'''Bard, Gunslinger, Rogue''' You've developed keen awareness and attention to detail. Your proficiency rank for Perception increases to master.

'''Commander''' You've developed the ability to see a scenario from every side. Your proficiency rank for Perception increases to master.

'''Exemplar''' Through your adventures, you've developed keen awareness and attention to detail, the better to watch over your wards and keep an eye out for monsters and other threats. Your proficiency rank for Perception increases to master.

'''Investigator, Swashbuckler, Thaumaturge''' Through your adventures, you've developed keen awareness and attention to detail. Your proficiency rank for Perception increases to master.

'''Ranger''' You have highly developed acuity and attention to detail. Your proficiency rank for Perception increases to master.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Quick Repair" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Quick Repair''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: For you, the Repair activity loses the exploration trait and takes 1 minute instead of 10 minutes. If you're a master in Crafting, it takes 3 actions. If you're legendary, it takes 1 action.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Running Reload" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Running Reload''' <small>(gunslinger, ranger)</small>
: You can reload your weapon on the move. You Stride, Step, or Sneak, then Interact to reload.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/skills/targeting/crosshair-bars-yellow.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Slinger's Precision" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Slinger's Precision''' <small>(gunslinger)</small>
: You deal an extra +2 precision damage with Strikes made using non-repeating crossbows and you deal an extra 1d4 precision damage on Strikes using non-repeating firearms.

If you have gunslinging legend, you instead deal +3 additional precision damage with Strikes using non-repeating crossbows and an additional 1d6 precision damage with Strikes using non-repeating firearms.

If you are using a combination weapon whose ranged form is a firearm or crossbow, you use your proficiency with firearms and crossbows for attacks made with the melee configuration of that weapon.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/creatures/mammals/bull-horns-eyes-glowin-orange.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Stubborn" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Stubborn''' <small>(gunslinger)</small>
: You don't like being told what to do, and you don't give up. Your proficiency rank for Will saves increases to expert. When you fail, but don't critically fail, a Will save against an effect that would give you the controlled condition, you can attempt a second save against the effect at the start of your next turn. On a successful second save, the controlled condition ends, though any other effects remain. As normal, failing this second save doesn't allow you to attempt a third save on the subsequent turn.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Sword and Pistol" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Sword and Pistol''' <small>(gunslinger)</small>
: You're comfortable wielding a firearm or crossbow in one hand and a melee weapon in the other, combining melee attacks with shots from the firearm. When you make a successful ranged Strike against an enemy within your reach with your one-handed firearm or one-handed crossbow, that enemy is Off-Guard against your next melee attack with a one-handed melee weapon.

When you make a successful melee Strike against an enemy with your one-handed melee weapon, the next ranged Strike you make against that enemy with a one-handed firearm or one-handed crossbow doesn't trigger reactions that would trigger on a ranged attack but not a melee attack, such as Reactive Strike. Either of these benefits is lost if not used by the end of your next turn.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Toughness" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Toughness''' <small>(general)</small>
: Your body can withstand more punishment than most before succumbing. Increase your maximum Hit Points by your level. You reduce the DC of recovery checks by 1.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Tumbling Teamwork" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Tumbling Teamwork''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: Your tumbling distracts a foe enough to create an advantage for one of your allies. When you successfully Tumble Through an enemy's space, an ally who is adjacent to that enemy can Step as a reaction, but they must remain adjacent to that enemy.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Ward Medic" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Ward Medic''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: You've studied in large medical wards, treating several patients at once and tending to all their needs. When you use Treat Disease or Treat Wounds, you can treat up to two targets. If you're a master in Medicine, you can treat up to four targets, and if you're legendary, you can treat up to eight targets.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/features/classes/way-of-the-drifter.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Way of the Drifter" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Way of the Drifter''' <small>(gunslinger)</small>
: You're a wanderer traveling from land to land with your gun and a melee weapon as company. Maybe you learned to fight with blade and pistol as a Shackles pirate, mastered the hand cannon and katana in Minkai, or practiced with a hatchet and clan pistol in Dongun Hold. You win battles by relying on mobility and flexible use of your weapons.

'''Slinger's Reload''' Reloading Strike

'''Deeds''' ''Initial'' Into the Fray; ''Advanced'' Finish The Job; ''Greater'' Drifter's Wake

'''Way Skill''' Acrobatics
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/features/classes/weapon-specialization.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Weapon Specialization" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Weapon Specialization''' <small>(alchemist, animist, bard, champion, cleric, commander, druid, fighter, gunslinger, inventor, investigator, kineticist, ranger, rogue, sorcerer, summoner, swashbuckler, witch, wizard)</small>
: You've learned how to inflict greater injuries with the weapons you know best. You deal an additional 2 damage with weapons and unarmed attacks in which you are an expert. This damage increases to 3 if you're a master, and 4 if you're legendary.

=====Actions=====
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/Passive.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Avoid Notice" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Avoid Notice''' <small>(exploration)</small>
: You attempt a Stealth check to avoid notice while traveling at half speed. If you're Avoiding Notice at the start of an encounter, you usually roll a Stealth check instead of a Perception check both to determine your initiative and to see if the enemies notice you (based on their Perception DCs, as normal for Sneak, regardless of their initiative check results).
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/OneAction.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Create a Diversion" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Create a Diversion''' <small>(mental)</small>
: With a {gesture}, a {trick}, or some {distracting words}, you can create a diversion that draws creatures' attention elsewhere. If you use a gesture or trick, this action gains the manipulate trait. If you use distracting words, it gains the auditory and linguistic traits.

Attempt a single Deception check and compare it to the Perception DCs of the creatures whose attention you're trying to divert. Whether or not you succeed, creatures you attempt to divert gain a +4 circumstance bonus to their Perception DCs against your attempts to Create a Diversion for 1 minute.

'''Success''' You become Hidden to each creature whose Perception DC is less than or equal to your result. (The hidden condition allows you to Sneak away.) This lasts until the end of your turn or until you do anything except Step or use the Stealth skill to Hide or Sneak. If you Strike a creature, the creature remains Off-Guard against that attack, and you then become observed. If you do anything else, you become observed just before you act unless the GM determines otherwise.

'''Failure''' You don't divert the attention of any creatures whose Perception DC exceeds your result, and those creatures are aware you were trying to trick them.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/FreeAction.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Into the Fray" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Into the Fray''' <small>(gunslinger)</small>
: '''Trigger''' You roll initiative.

You know trouble can lurk around every corner, and your hands never stray far from your holsters. You can Interact to draw a one-handed ranged weapon and can then Interact to draw a one-handed melee weapon. As your first action on your first turn, you can Stride as a free action toward an enemy you can perceive. If you can't perceive any enemies or can't end your movement closer to one, you can't use this Stride.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/OneAction.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Reloading Strike" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Reloading Strike''' <small>(gunslinger)</small>
: '''Requirements''' You're wielding a firearm or crossbow in one hand, and your other hand either wields a one-handed melee weapon or is empty.

You make a melee attack and reload in one fluid movement. Strike an opponent within reach with your one-handed melee weapon (or, if your other hand is empty, with an unarmed attack), and then Interact to reload. You don't need a free hand to reload in this way and this reload does not trigger reactions.

</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>🎒 Inventory</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
=====Bags & Storage=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/containers/bags/pack-leather-white-tan.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Backpack" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Backpack]] || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 sp
|-
| colspan="6" |
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/survival/rope-wrapped-brown.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Rope" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Rope
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/adventuring-gear/chalk.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Chalk" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Chalk ×10
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/lights/torch-brown.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Torch" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Torch ×5
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/consumables/other-consumables/holy-water.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Holy Water" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Holy Water ×2
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/adventuring-gear/waterskin.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Waterskin" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Waterskin
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/containers/ammunition/quiver-simple-brown.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Bedroll" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Bedroll
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/commodities/stone/geode-raw-tan.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Flint and Steel" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Flint and Steel
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/adventuring-gear/rations.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Rations" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Rations ×2
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/survival/soap.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Soap" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Soap
* <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/commodities/tech/pipe-tin.webp" width="16" height="16" alt="Weapon Siphon" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Weapon Siphon
|}

</div>
</div>

''Last synced: 2026-06-20 01:33''
[[Category:Characters]]
